# Pytest Benchmark

## GLASS

:::: {layout="[60,40]"}

::: {.cell}

Generator for Large Scale Structure

- Python code which generates full-universe simulations for large galaxy
  surveys.
- Heavily depends on NumPy.

:::

::: {.cell}

![](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/glass-dev/glass/refs/heads/main/docs/_static/spheres.png)

:::

::::

:::: {.fragment}

### Project aim

Port GLASS to the array-api such that NumPy could be replaced by the user with
any other array-api compatible library including one that is GPU enabled.

::::

## The Python Array-API (i)

Before - Libraries limit the tools available

![Credit: [Aaron Meurer, ‘Python Array API Standard’, SciPy 2023](https://youtu.be/16rB-fosAWw) via [Lucas Colley, A Hitchhiker’s Guide to the Array API Standard Ecosystem, EuroSciPy 2025](https://lucascolley.github.io/talks/euroscipy-25-array-api/)](./_assets/array-ecosystem.png){.r-stretch}

## The Python Array-API (ii)

After - All libraries work with all tools

![Credit: [Aaron Meurer, ‘Python Array API Standard’, SciPy 2023](https://youtu.be/16rB-fosAWw) via [Lucas Colley, A Hitchhiker’s Guide to the Array API Standard Ecosystem, EuroSciPy 2025](https://lucascolley.github.io/talks/euroscipy-25-array-api/)](./_assets/ecosystem-modified.png){.r-stretch}

## Why did we need benchmarks? {.r-fit-text}

Porting to the array-api adds performance penalties.

```{.py code-line-numbers="-1|1,4,9|5-6"}
import array_api_compat

def do_something(x, y):
    xp = array_api_compat.array_namespace(x, y, use_compat=False)
    if (xp.__name__ == "numpy"):
      # Do something NumPy specific...

def do_something_else(x):
    xp = x.__array_namespace__()
    #...
```

::: {.fragment}

- Extra if conditions
- Extra function calls
- Transforming large arrays between backends

:::
